name: Eminem
description: Slim Shady, Marshall, the Real Slim Shady — the Detroit rapper's records and lore.

questions.q: What is Eminem's real name?

source.notes: 'Eminem' is a phonetic spelling of his initials M&M (Marshall Mathers).

questions.q: Eminem grew up in (or near) which Michigan city, referenced in '8 Mile'?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed misleading 'suburb' framing; born in St. Joseph, Missouri but raised in Detroit (and Warren, a suburb).

questions.q: What was Eminem's debut studio album, released in 1996 with limited press?

source.notes: Released independently before Dr. Dre signed him.

questions.q: Which 1999 album was Eminem's mainstream breakthrough?

source.notes: Won Best Rap Album Grammy in 2000.

questions.q: Which legendary producer signed Eminem to Aftermath/Interscope in 1998?

source.notes: Dr. Dre heard his demo and signed him.

questions.q: Which 2002 song from '8 Mile' won Eminem the Best Original Song Oscar?

source.notes: First hip-hop song to win Best Original Song. Eminem famously skipped the ceremony.

questions.q: Which iconic 2000 Eminem track features a fan obsessing about him in a series of letters?

source.notes: Featured Dido. The name 'Stan' became dictionary slang for an obsessive fan.

questions.q: Which 2002 semi-autobiographical Eminem film grossed $242M and starred Kim Basinger as his mother?

source.notes: Directed by Curtis Hanson.

questions.q: Which 2010 collab with Rihanna was Eminem's biggest single?

source.notes: Reached #1 in 25+ countries. From the album 'Recovery'.

questions.q: Which 2010 album was Eminem's comeback after his 2008 hiatus?

source.notes: Featured 'Not Afraid' and 'Love the Way You Lie.'

questions.q: Which 2013 Eminem track is widely cited as one of the fastest rap songs of all time at 9.6 syllables/sec?

source.notes: From The Marshall Mathers LP 2 (2013).

questions.q: Which 2018 Eminem album, named after a Japanese term, took aim at his critics?

source.notes: Surprise-released August 2018.

questions.q: What is the name of Eminem's longtime ex-wife referenced in many songs?

source.notes: Kim Anne Scott (later Mathers); married/divorced twice. Hailie is their daughter.

questions.q: What is the name of Eminem's daughter, frequently referenced in his music?

source.notes: Hailie Jade Mathers, born December 25, 1995.

questions.q: Which Eminem-fronted Detroit hip-hop collective released albums in 2001 and 2017?

source.notes: D12 (Detroit Twelve) — also featured Proof, Bizarre, Kuniva, Swift, Kon Artis.

questions.q: Which late D12 member and Eminem's best friend was tragically shot in 2006?

source.notes: Proof (DeShaun Holton). Killed at a Detroit nightclub.

questions.q: How many Grammy Awards has Eminem won (as of 2024)?

source.notes: 15 Grammy wins as of 2024 — most for a rapper at the time, since surpassed by Kendrick.

questions.q: Which 2024 Eminem album, his 12th, signaled the death of his Slim Shady alter ego?
choices: The Death of Slim Shady (Coup de Grâce)

source.notes: Released July 12, 2024.

questions.q: Which 2002 single's video features Eminem mocking pop stars including Britney Spears and Moby?

source.notes: From The Eminem Show (2002).

questions.q: Which Eminem alter ego is described as the most uncensored, dark version of himself?

source.notes: Slim Shady debuted on the 1997 Slim Shady EP.
